name: Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop
name_original: Campo de Gómara por el Castillo de Almenar de Sória — circular desde Gómara
description: This 56.4-mile (90.7 km) moderate road cycling route from Gómara explores the Campo de Gómara and passes the historic Almenar de Soria Castl
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Gómara traverse a landscape characterized by open plains and rolling hills, offering routes suitable for moderate endurance. The region features rural roads connecting small villages and historical sites. Cyclists can expect varied terrain with gradual ascents and descents across the Gómara countryside. Best road cycling routes around Gómara The most popular road cycling route is Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop, a 56.4 miles (90.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete, passing by Almenar de Soria Castle and through open fields. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Serón de Nágima and Monteag, a moderate 39.6 miles (63.8 km) path, offering views of rural villages and rolling terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop, a 46.2 miles (74.3 km) trail leading through the Alto de la Cruz de Deza, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Gómara is defined by open plains, rolling hills, and rural roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, primarily focusing on moderate routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Gómara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Gómara?
A: Road cycling routes around Gómara feature a landscape of open plains and rolling hills. You'll encounter varied terrain with gradual ascents and descents, primarily on rural roads connecting small villages and historical sites.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or advanced cyclists in Gómara?
A: The road cycling routes around Gómara are primarily moderate in difficulty, making them suitable for cyclists with some endurance. There are no routes specifically classified as easy or difficult in this selection, focusing on a balanced experience across the Gómara countryside.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes available in Gómara?
A: For longer rides, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079261523/monasterio-de-huerta-and-monteagudo-de-las-vicarias-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Huerta and Monteagudo de las Vicarías Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 94.7 km (58.8 miles) with significant elevation gain. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079256327/almenar-de-soria-castle-campo-de-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, spanning about 90.7 km (56.4 miles).

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Gómara?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Gómara are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079256327/almenar-de-soria-castle-campo-de-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077377818/alto-de-la-cruz-de-deza-and-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079261523/monasterio-de-huerta-and-monteagudo-de-las-vicarias-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Huerta and Monteagudo de las Vicarías Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Gómara?
A: Several routes pass by notable historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079256327/almenar-de-soria-castle-campo-de-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore routes that lead through charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma de Soria&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribarroya&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's heritage.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often provide scenic views of the rural countryside and rolling terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077377818/alto-de-la-cruz-de-deza-and-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt; leads through the Alto de la Cruz de Deza, which offers elevated perspectives of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Gómara?
A: Gómara experiences a continental climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cold, so plan accordingly for those seasons.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Gómara area?
A: There are currently 6 road cycling routes detailed for the Gómara area, all of which are classified as moderate in difficulty.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Gómara town center?
A: Yes, some routes are designed to pass through or near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gómara Town Center&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to experience the local atmosphere and potentially find amenities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077377818/alto-de-la-cruz-de-deza-and-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that includes Gómara.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on road cycling routes in Gómara?
A: The routes around Gómara feature significant, yet gradual, elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077377818/alto-de-la-cruz-de-deza-and-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cruz de Deza and Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 800 meters, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079256327/almenar-de-soria-castle-campo-de-gomara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenar de Soria Castle - Campo de Gómara Loop&lt;/a&gt; involves around 720 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes in Gómara?
A: The routes in Gómara connect small villages, which may offer local cafes or small shops for refreshments. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer stretches, as services might be sparse between towns. Planning your route to pass through larger villages like Gómara or Serón de Nágima can increase your chances of finding amenities.
tips: Borobia is a small, picturesque village that has found its appeal to tourists thanks to its clear skies, free of light pollution. This makes it ideal for stargazing.

Hinojosa del Campo is a small agricultural center of traditional houses that sprout among the wheat ears of the surrounding fields.

From afar you can see the 10th century Berber tower that today forms part of the fortified Romanesque church of La Asunción de Nuestra Señora, from the 12th century. This curious temple has another tower that preserves its Romanesque characteristics.
On a low hill in the southwest part of the town we find this castle that strategically dominates the surroundings. It is one of the best preserved castles in the province of Soria.
Beautiful road that runs between these two towns in the middle of agricultural fields and a flat area with little gradient.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting the Campo de Gómara circular route?
faqs.answer: As this route starts and ends in Gómara, you should be able to find parking within the town. Look for public parking areas or suitable street parking near the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily traverses the Campo de Gómara region, which is characterized by agricultural landscapes and rolling hills. For racebike cycling, you can expect mostly paved surfaces. While the Soria province has varied terrain, this specific route is designed for road cycling, implying a focus on well-maintained roads.

faqs.question: What notable sights will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This route offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the settlements of Borobia and Hinojosa del Campo. A major highlight is the Almenar de Soria Castle, a 15th-century historical site. The route also includes a section described as From Almenar de Soria to Cabrejas del Campo, which is a dedicated cycle way.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Soria province generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat and winter cold. Spring (April-June) and Autumn (September-October) are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery. Always check the local weather forecast before you head out.

faqs.question: Is the Campo de Gómara by the Castillo de Almenar de Sória route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas, this is a racebike route primarily on public roads. It's best to consider the safety of your dog and other road users. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are well-behaved, on a leash when appropriate, and that you adhere to any local regulations regarding pets in towns or specific areas along the route.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and pathways. However, if you plan to visit the interior of the Almenar de Soria Castle, there might be an entrance fee for the castle itself, so it's advisable to check their official website for current information.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several settlements, including Borobia and Hinojosa del Campo, as well as starting in Gómara. These towns may offer small cafes, bars, or shops where you can refuel. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer rides, as services might be spaced out.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with or run alongside other paths. Notably, it covers small portions of the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo (Valcorba - Ciria) and the broader Santander-Mediterráneo route. You'll also briefly encounter the Camino de Valdeconejos.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its distance of over 90 km and moderate difficulty, this route is generally more suited for experienced racebike cyclists rather than families with young children or beginners. While the terrain is mostly paved, the length and potential for rolling hills require a good level of fitness. For family-friendly options, you might look for shorter, flatter routes in the Soria province.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling the Campo de Gómara route?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and beautiful scenery. The route takes you through the agricultural landscapes of the Campo de Gómara, characterized by open fields and rolling hills. The Soria province is known for its natural beauty, including forests, valleys, and riverbanks, offering a varied backdrop to your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a racebike tour on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate racebike tour, essential items include your racebike in good condition, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire (including layers for changing weather), sufficient water and snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), a phone for emergencies, and navigation (like the komoot app) to follow the route. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended.

waypoints.name: From Almenar de Soria to Cabrejas del Campo
